architecture_notes: KitchenSync is a single Rust CLI binary: one controller drives the run lifecycle and the rest are focused services it calls.
Local file:// and SFTP peers sit behind one transport interface (facade with per-scheme adapters), so sync logic never branches on scheme and sees uniform error categories.
Dry-run is a cross-cutting mode threaded into the components that touch peers, not re-implemented per call site.
All output goes to stdout through one verbosity-governed component; stderr stays empty.
Concurrency is bounded by one global copy-slot limit shared across the whole run.

A component that owns private sub-crates builds them itself. Its constructor takes only the shared sibling services it legitimately depends on (for example Snapshot takes Transport), never a sub-crate type, and no public operation's parameter or return type names a sub-crate type. Those sub-crate types stay entirely behind the owning component's boundary, so a caller depends only on the component and never on its internal sub-crates. This governs every facade with internal helpers -- Transport, Snapshot, SyncEngine, and CopyQueue -- because a caller cannot name a sub-crate that is only a transitive dependency (cargo resolver v2 does not expose it), so leaking such a type makes the component impossible to construct or call.

children.name: Cli
children.responsibility: Parse the command line, validate peers and option values, render the verbatim help screen, and produce the validated run configuration or a rejection with the help text.

children.name: Transport
children.responsibility: Provide uniform per-peer filesystem operations over local and SFTP schemes, including URL normalization, SFTP authentication and host-key checks, fallback-URL connection establishment, root creation, and shared error categories.

children.name: Snapshot
children.responsibility: Own each peer's SQLite snapshot database: schema, path hashing, monotonic timestamps, per-peer row reads and updates, tombstones, opportunistic maintenance, and SWAP-staged download and upload of snapshot.db.

children.name: SyncEngine
children.responsibility: Drive the recursive pre-order tree walk: parallel listing, entry union, exclude filtering, per-peer classification against snapshots, the canon/subordinate/bidirectional and type-conflict decision rules, directory creates, and inline displacement to BAK.

children.name: CopyQueue
children.responsibility: Execute enqueued file copies concurrently under the global copy-slot limit with per-copy retry tracking, SWAP-staged replacement, mod_time setting, BAK archival, and TMP/SWAP/BAK staging cleanup and recovery.

children.name: RunController
children.responsibility: Orchestrate the run end to end: connect peers concurrently, gate on reachability and canon, drive the traversal and copy phases, write snapshots back, report completion, and own the run-completion exit code, honoring dry-run throughout.

children.name: Output
children.responsibility: Emit all program output to stdout per verbosity level: per-action C/X progress lines, error and info diagnostics, and trace-level copy-slot events, while keeping stderr empty.
